A daily round-up of showbiz news and gossip from around the world, focusing on celebrities, movies, music and entertainment.

programmes.show_name: Rise of the Footsoldier: Extreme & Extended
programmes.show_description: Crime thriller starring Craig Fairbrass and Terry Stone. A ruthless Essex gangster plots revenge against the drug baron who betrayed him and left him in prison. (2007)
In Suffolk, a series of farm thefts wreak havoc until CCTV provides the vital clues police need to crack the case. Plus shocking footage of the killing of protected wildlife.
Clip show of explosive confrontations, presented by George Lamb. Whether raging at a cheating partner or a figure of authority, women are physically venting their anger.
Monica invites all those without prior plans to Thanksgiving dinner, but soon wishes she hadn't. Joey gets some embarrassing modelling work.

Chandler accidentally sees Rachel in the nude and is shocked when she insists that he return the favour. Phoebe's boyfriend antagonises the group.
On Valentine's Day, Ross meets his ex-wife while on his first date in nine years. Monica and Rachel rebel by burning mementos of past relationships.
Monica applies for the chef's position at a smart restaurant and cooks the manager a feast.
Joey and Chandler find out about Phoebe's twin sister. Ross attends childbirth classes with his ex-wife and her girlfriend.
A terrier-cross puppy named Teddy torments two cats named Ernie and Molly; a re-homed cat named Olive is afraid of a standard poodle named Eddie in Wales.
Groomer Marie takes her beloved Pekingese four pack to work - but they're scaring off the furry clientele. And a couple's screaming Frenchie is driving a wedge between them.
Can Graeme Hall stop a Cockerpoo's dirty obsession, and also curtail Ziggy from ransacking his owner's house? Plus, the basics of sit and stay - but without having to say a word.
Documentary series. A violent new crime wave known as County Lines is taking hold in Britain. Across the country, urban drug gangs are expanding their empires into small towns.
A fifth of the offences which North Yorkshire Police deal with, like theft and drug-dealing, are committed by crooks from outside the area.
In York, early-morning raids are carried out on two properties seeking suspects who are believed to be exploiting teenage girls.
A tyre stinger is used to stop a vehicle suspected of carrying stolen phones. Automatic Number Plate Recognition is used to track and intercept a vehicle.
Jim intercepts a car containing cannabis, a late-night reveller talks himself into trouble and Sergeant Baxter is confronted by a dishonest driver.
The interceptors stop an uninsured driver with a violent past. Jon Peacock and his dog Brad hunt for suspected burglars. Sarah Capes smells something illegal.
The hunt for a suspected burglar takes an unexpected turn, a late-night stop reveals a bag of drugs and Jon has a tussle with a fallen tree.
Dan is in hot pursuit of a two-wheeled tearaway, a drunk driver nearly causes a calamity and Mark deals with a three-car collision.

programmes.show_name: Crime of Passion? The Trial of Jane Andrews
programmes.show_description: The shocking trial that drove a wedge through a privileged set, in which the former dresser to the Duchess of York was accused of brutally murdering her boyfriend.
...Victim? Pt. 1. A man suffers a violent beating by his ex-wife's new partner. The brawl ends with the attacker's killing. Did the accused kill in self-defence or murder a love rival?
Tina Bowen believes her dead mother is playing tricks, until a battle with an intruder uncovers a real-life horror. And Amber Dawn's lights turn on and off until danger explodes one night. (Ep2)
When Guida Rufino met Anthony Roberts she finally got a baby. But only a few years later the couple would both end up dead in a frenzied attack that left a child without its parents.
The team's new dermatologist Dr Magnus Lynch specialises in facial surgery and makes an explosive start with a large jawline cyst. Plus, a man with infectious warts.
The hospital is in the middle of one of the worst storms in decades, but that doesn't stop casualty being busy. It's not long before paramedics bring in the storm's first victim.
The gang decide to make New Year's Eve a partner-free zone. But Ross gets around the ban by bringing his new companion - a pet monkey called Marcel.
Chandler's flamboyant mother visits New York, while Phoebe and Monica flirt with a handsome stranger while crossing a road and end up causing a traffic accident.

The Truscott family continue renovations at their hotel. And with invited guests expecting a lavish launch for the new leisure facility, will everything be ready in time?
John, who works 80 hours a week, takes a well-earned break on a family excursion to a bird sanctuary. But he must also face his fear of heights, travelling by cable car.
Graeme Hall returns. A deaf Dalmatian is ruining his owner's love life. A Married at First Sight star is given the runaround by the other love in his life, his Bantam Bulldog.
Road crime cops Nick Lovatt and Mark Scott from Derbyshire's Specialist ANPR crime unit target drug dealers coming into the county from other areas.
In Derbyshire, the cops are forced to make a dramatic stop during a race to stop burglars. And some stolen property turns up in an unusual place.
Jim responds to an urgent call for backup from a colleague, who is surrounded by an angry mob following a report of an agressive young man's dangerous driving.
Gordo and Dan unleash their tasers to help arrest a knife-wielding teenager, and Jim leads five units to box in a car suspected of carrying drugs.
Cases include a raid on a suspected drug user whose door won't budge, a chase after a souped-up hot hatch and a late-night reveller in Nantwich town centre.
The Interceptors come under attack from a gang of violent, armed robbers, and the boys in the 'Big Blue Van' respond to reports of bottles raining down on the streets.

programmes.show_name: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car...
programmes.show_description: ...Back! Following car recovery companies that are tasked with taking illegal, unroadworthy, untaxed, uninsured vehicles off the road and ensuring their owners pay the penalty.
Mackenzie remains loyal to Tane. Leah supports Lacey. Will David be able to protect his daughter?
John's DIY goes awry. David cops it from all sides. Cash hands over what he's been hiding.
Dr Sian Morris suspects that long-term patient Allan's recent dramatic weight loss is a result of his addiction to crack cocaine.
It's another gruelling shift for team at Barnsley Hospital's Casualty Department. Juggling a back-log of walk-in patients and a constant stream of new ambulance emergencies.
A 52-year-old man complains of neck and shoulder pains after being thrown off his motorbike, and a 40-year-old epileptic woman has collapsed with a dangerously low breathing rate.
A new series returns as we follow the brave A&E night-shift teams in Belfast, Newham and Norwich. At Norfolk and Norwich Hospital, a stabbing sends both victim and attacker to A&E.
